title: myristica fatua subsp. fatua
intro: Myristica fatua subsp. fatua, also known as the false nutmeg, is a plant species belonging to the family Myristicaceae. It is a small tree or shrub that is native to tropical Africa and is often confused with the true nutmeg (Myristica fragrans) due to its similar appearance and scent. The seeds of Myristica fatua subsp. fatua have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The seeds can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, and are also used in traditional African medicine to promote digestion, relieve pain, and reduce inflammation.
